title: Fertility rate vs. average years of schooling
subtitle: The [total fertility rate](#dod:fertility-rate), in a given year. Before 2015, the share without education are based on estimates; from 2015 onwards, they are based on projections.
caption:
Fertility rate vs. average years of schooling

The total fertility rate, in a given year. Before 2015, the share without education are based on estimates; from 2015 onwards, they are based on projections.

This scatter plot displays the relationship between total fertility rate and average years of schooling for the year 2020. The vertical axis measures the total fertility rate in live births per woman, ranging from 0 to over 6, while the horizontal axis represents average years of schooling in 15-64 year olds, spanning from roughly 3 to 13 years. Each circle represents a country, with size proportional to population and color indicating the continent. A clear negative correlation is visible: countries with lower average years of schooling generally exhibit higher fertility rates. Niger and the Democratic Republic of Congo appear at the top left, with fertility rates near or above 6 and schooling averages below 5 years. Other African nations such as Mozambique, Sudan, and Tanzania cluster in the upper-left quadrant with fertility rates between 4 and 5 and schooling between 4 and 7 years. In contrast, countries in Europe and North America, including Italy, Poland, Japan, and the United States, are positioned in the bottom right, showing fertility rates between 1 and 2 and schooling averages exceeding 11 years. Large population centers like India, China, and Brazil sit in the middle of the distribution, with fertility rates between 1 and 2.5 and schooling averages ranging from 7 to 9 years. The legend distinguishes regions including North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania through specific color coding.
